mechanism_narrative: An always-on rule that maps higher 3-month PAYEMS growth to higher next-20-day S&P 500 returns: payroll expansion raises household income and expected sales, so positive payroll momentum produces positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:latest PAYEMS snapshot value was 144747.0 thousand as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if the rolling 3-month PAYEMS growth coefficient on 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is non-positive over two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.25 percentage point or larger 3-month rise in FEDFUNDS tightens discount rates and financing conditions, reducing equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 0.25pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest FEDFUNDS snapshot value was 0.4 percent as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered tightening episodes have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations or a non-negative average return.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in RSAFS and HOUST identifies broad household demand strength; when retail sales and housing starts both rise over 3 months, expected corporate revenues improve and S&P 500 returns are positive over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both cross above their 3-month moving averages
evidence_refs: fact:latest RSAFS snapshot value was 459821.0 million dollars and latest HOUST value was 1142.0 thousand units
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-HOUST trigger does not outperform the unconditional 60-day return by at least 1 percentage point on average in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A downside break in INDPRO captures weakening real production and margin pressure; when industrial production turns negative versus its recent trend, earnings revisions deteriorate and S&P 500 returns are negative over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its 6-month moving average and its 6-month change is negative
evidence_refs: fact:latest INDPRO snapshot value was 104.4016 as of two months before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO downside breaks are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%, real policy accommodation fails to offset inflation risk; margin and rate uncertainty lower valuation multiples, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized inflation crosses above 2.5% while FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:latest CPIAUCSL snapshot value was 240.305 and latest FEDFUNDS value was 0.4
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS interaction term has a non-negative coefficient or if triggered episodes earn positive average 20-day returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in UNRATE indicates labor-market slack and rising recession risk; when unemployment rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, expected earnings fall and S&P 500 returns are negative over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest UNRATE snapshot value was 5.0 percent as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-rise triggers are followed by non-negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if hit-rate for negative returns is below 50% over rolling 10-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS contracts and the latest GDPC1 growth is weak, consumer demand weakness is broad enough to cut near-term earnings expectations; this demand shock produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change crosses below 0% while latest GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth is below 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:latest GDPC1 snapshot value was 16583.1 billions of chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: Reject if the combined RSAFS-GDPC1 trigger has a 60-day forward return not lower than the unconditional sample mean in at least 70% of walk-forward folds.

mechanism_narrative: A positive surprise-like crossing in HOUST indicates stronger construction activity and durable-goods demand; when housing starts move above their 12-month average, cyclicals reprice upward and S&P 500 returns are positive over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above its 12-month moving average from below
evidence_refs: fact:latest HOUST snapshot value was 1142.0 thousand units as of two months before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST upside crossings do not produce positive average 5-day S&P 500 returns or if the signal hit-rate is below 50% in rolling 5-year tests.
